steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the phone's home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Phone, Messages, Chrome, and Settings. The Google search bar is at the bottom. The goal is to turn off Bluetooth, which requires accessing the system settings.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open the Settings app → | Step 2: Navigate to Connected devices or Bluetooth settings | Step 3: Toggle Bluetooth off, task complete

[Decision]
To turn off Bluetooth, I need to access the device's system settings. Since I am on the home screen and the Settings app icon is clearly visible in the bottom right corner of the app dock, I will open it to proceed with finding the Bluetooth controls.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The current screen is the main menu of the "Settings" app. Visible options include "Network & internet", "Connected devices" (which has a subtitle "Bluetooth, pairing"), "Apps", "Notifications", "Battery", "Storage", and "Sound & vibration".

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Connected devices or Bluetooth settings → | Step 3: Toggle Bluetooth off, task complete

[Decision]
Based on the current plan, the next step is to navigate to the Bluetooth settings. On the screen, the "Connected devices" option explicitly mentions "Bluetooth, pairing" in its description. Therefore, I need to tap on "Connected devices" to find the Bluetooth toggle.

[Memory]
Navigating through Settings to find Bluetooth controls. The "Connected devices" menu is the entry point for Bluetooth settings.

[Observation]
The current screen is "Connected devices". Visible options include "Pair new device", "Saved devices" -> "See all", and "Connection preferences" which has a subtitle "Bluetooth, Android Auto". There is no direct Bluetooth toggle switch on this screen.

[Reflection]
The previous plan assumed Step 3 would be directly toggling Bluetooth off after entering "Connected devices", but the toggle is not here; it requires navigating deeper into "Connection preferences" because Bluetooth settings are nested.

[Replan]
Step 1: Open Settings ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Connected devices ✓ | Step 3: Access Connection preferences to find Bluetooth settings → | Step 4: Toggle Bluetooth off, task complete

[Decision]
Based on the updated plan, I need to find the Bluetooth settings. The "Connection preferences" option explicitly lists "Bluetooth" in its description. Therefore, I must tap on "Connection preferences" to proceed to the next menu where the Bluetooth toggle should be located.

[Memory]
Navigating Settings -> Connected devices -> Connection preferences. The direct Bluetooth toggle is not on the main "Connected devices" screen.

[Observation]
The current screen is "Connection preferences". The visible options include "Bluetooth", "Cast" (Not connected), "Printing" (1 print service on), "Chromebook" (Your phone is not linked to a Chromebook), "Nearby Share", and "Android Auto". The "Bluetooth" option is at the top of the list.

[Reflection]
The previous plan assumed Step 4 would be directly toggling Bluetooth off, but the current screen requires entering the "Bluetooth" menu first before the toggle switch appears.

[Replan]
Step 1-3: Navigate to Connection preferences ✓ | Step 4: Enter Bluetooth settings → | Step 5: Toggle Bluetooth off, task complete

[Decision]
Based on the Replan, the current goal is to enter the Bluetooth settings. The "Bluetooth" option is clearly visible at the top of the "Connection preferences" list. I need to tap on "Bluetooth" to proceed to the screen where the toggle switch is located.

[Memory]
Navigating Settings -> Connected devices -> Connection preferences -> Bluetooth. The direct toggle switch is not on the Connection preferences screen; it requires entering the specific Bluetooth menu.

[Observation]
The current screen is the "Bluetooth" settings page. The main element is the "Use Bluetooth" toggle switch, which is currently in the ON position (indicated by the switch being on the right side and highlighted). Below it, the device name is "sdk_gphone64_x86_64", and there is an option to "Pair new device".

[Plan Update]
Step 1-4: Enter Bluetooth settings ✓ | Step 5: Toggle Bluetooth off → | Step 6: Task complete

[Decision]
Based on the Plan Update, the current goal is to toggle Bluetooth off. The "Use Bluetooth" switch is clearly visible and currently turned on. I need to tap this switch to turn it off, which will complete the user's request.

[Memory]
Navigating Settings -> Connected devices -> Connection preferences -> Bluetooth. Reached the Bluetooth settings page. The "Use Bluetooth" toggle is currently ON.

[Observation]
The current screen is the "Bluetooth" settings page. The "Use Bluetooth" toggle switch is now in the OFF position (greyed out and switched to the left). The "Pair new device" option below it is disabled (greyed out text saying "Bluetooth will turn on to pair").

[Plan Update]
Step 1-4: Enter Bluetooth settings ✓ | Step 5: Toggle Bluetooth off ✓ | Step 6: Task complete →
